_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an average index of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice prices set at 100 in 2015, currently stands at 132, reflecting a 32% increase since 2015. This indicates a notable rise in the overall cost of these common breakfast-related commodities. The increase has been uneven across the basket components and is driven primarily by recent developments rather than a steady climb over time. Such variation highlights the complexity behind the aggregate index movement.
notes.movers: Coffee has seen the most significant increase at 93% since 2015, followed by orange juice rising 22%. Wheat and sugar prices have experienced much smaller gains, 7% and 5% respectively. These price changes are largely caused by specific weather events and crop diseases—such as frost and drought affecting arabica coffee regions, as well as issues like citrus greening and hurricanes impacting orange juice production. These thin soft commodity markets are highly sensitive to such localized disruptions rather than broad economic inflation trends.
notes.why: The headline rise in the breakfast basket is driven mainly by a few weather-exposed crops, particularly coffee and orange juice. Because these are sensitive to environmental factors and disease, their inputs remain volatile and can stay elevated for multiple years, impacting prices intermittently rather than gradually.
